original_hypothesis.title: extendSelfStake / extendCommunityStake can be used to lock users into longer exposure windows without adding stake (operational griefing via forced long-duration locks in shared accounts)
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: extendSelfStake() and extendCommunityStake() allow extending the unlockTime without adding stake. If an address is operationally shared (e.g., Safe, custodial wallet, or compromised signer with limited permissions), a malicious co-signer/operator could continuously extend unlock times to keep funds exposed to slashing for longer and prevent timely withdrawals, creating extended penalty windows.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Not an on-chain permission bypass (only msg.sender can extend its own stakes), but operationally realistic when stake addresses are multisigs/shared hot wallets or automation agents. This can be used as a griefing lever to keep funds locked/exposed.
deployment_assumptions: Stake addresses are controlled by multiple parties (multisig) or an automation key that could behave maliciously
Operational policy permits any co-controller to extend locks
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Project includes Safe tooling (utils/safe.ts) indicating multisig-based operations are in use; no explicit policy evidence for stake address custody.

proof_of_signal.pattern: extendSelfStake: only checks amount > 0 then sets unlockTime = block.timestamp + duration; no requirement to add stake

preconditions: Attacker can submit transactions from the same address that owns the stake (shared custody / compromised signer)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Lock extension is unilateral by the staker address and costs no additional stake, creating a cheap griefing vector in shared-control setups.
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Any time before withdrawal; can be repeated.

operational_questions: Are stakes expected to be held by EOAs or by multisigs/shared addresses?
Do operational controls prevent a single actor from extending locks unilaterally?
Is there monitoring for repeated lock extensions?
validation_requirements: Review custody model and signer permissions for any stake-holding addresses
Simulate repeated extensions and measure operational impact (delayed withdrawals, extended slashing exposure)
Define alerting for abnormal unlockTime changes

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Obtain ability to transact as the staker address (shared signer or limited compromise)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Call extendSelfStake/extendCommunityStake with max duration repeatedly
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Keep funds locked and exposed to slashing while preventing planned withdrawals

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Medium: delayed withdrawals and prolonged exposure to slashing penalties
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium: requires governance over address custody/signers, not contract state
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: Treasury/user fund management for entities using shared accounts

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Frequent SelfStake/CommunityStake events with amount=0 and increasing unlockTime
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Withdrawals unexpectedly failing due to new lock times
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Extensions clustered around planned withdrawal dates

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
